title: Morgan Neville’s Mister Rogers Documentary Reminds Us to Be a Good Neighbor
format: Sean Fennessey talks with Morgan Neville about his Mr. Rogers documentary, radical kindness in an uncivil moment, and why documentary went from the spinach of filmmaking to a boom.

films.title: Won't You Be My Neighbor?
films.note: Neville's Mr. Rogers film — pitched as a movie about ideas, not a biography. He boils Rogers down to "radical kindness" and argues its opposite is fear. Joanne Rogers' instruction: don't make Fred a saint.

films.title: 20 Feet from Stardom
films.note: His Oscar winner, on backup singers. The win cut his fundraising from 50% of his time to 6%, which is why he can be this prolific.

films.title: Best of Enemies
films.note: The Gore Vidal–William F. Buckley debates; Neville calls it a mirror to the Rogers film — both about civility and common ground. Nobody wanted to fund "two old dead white guys talking on TV" until after the Oscar.

films.title: They'll Love Me When I'm Dead
films.note: His next film, on Orson Welles' last 15 years back in Hollywood, made with Frank Marshall.

films.title: The Other Side of the Wind
films.note: Welles' unfinished film about a director who can't finish a movie; the dailies sat in a Paris vault 42 years before Neville and Marshall liberated them.

They'll Love Me When I'm Dead.title: They'll Love Me When I'm Dead

The Other Side of the Wind.title: The Other Side of the Wind
